title: dianthus arenarius subsp. pseudosquarrosus
intro: Dianthus arenarius subsp. pseudosquarrosus, also known as the 'Pseudosquarrosus' or 'False Squarrose Pink', is a subspecies of the Dianthus arenarius, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Dianthus arenarius subsp. pseudosquarrosus are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
